Authors: Abdelnabi, Sahar; Gomaa, Amr; Sivaprasad, Sarath; Schönherr, Lea; Fritz, Mario
Title: LLM-Deliberation: Evaluating LLMs with Interactive Multi-Agent Negotiation Games
Abstract: We propose using scorable negotiation games as a new evaluation framework for LLMs. We create a testbed of diverse text-based multi-agent multi-issue semantically rich negotiation games. Via a systematic zero-shot Chain-of-Thought prompting we show that agents can negotiate and consistently reach successful deals.

Authors: Akata, Elif; Schulz, Lion; Coda-Forno, Julian; Oh, Seong Joon; Bethge, Matthias; Schulz, Eric
Title: Playing repeated games with large language models
Abstract: We propose to use behavioural game theory to study LLMs cooperation and coordination behaviour. We let different LLMs play finitely repeated 2x2 games with each other with human-like strategies and actual human players. LLMs perform particularly well at self-interested games like the iterated Prisoner's Dilemma family but behave suboptimally in coordination games like Battle of the Sexes.

Authors: Bakhtin, Anton; Brown, Noam; Dinan, Emily; Farina, Gabriele; Flaherty, Colin; Fried, Daniel; Goff, Andrew; Gray, Jonathan; Hu, Hengyuan; Jacob, Athul Paul; Komeili, Mojtaba; Konath, Karthik; Kwon, Minae; Lerer, Adam; Lewis, Mike; Miller, Alexander H.; Mitts, Sandra; Renduchintala, Adithya; Roller, Stephen; Rowe, Dirk; Shi, Weiyan; Spisak, Joe; Wei, Alexander; Wu, David J.; Zhang, Hugh; Zijlstra, Markus
Title: Human-level play in the game of Diplomacy by combining language models with strategic reasoning
Abstract: We introduce Cicero the first AI agent to achieve human-level performance in Diplomacy a strategy game involving cooperation and competition that emphasizes natural language negotiation. Across 40 games Cicero achieved more than double the average score of human players and ranked in the top 10%.

Authors: Davidson, Tim R.; Veselovsky, Veniamin; Josifoski, Martin; Peyrard, Maxime; Bosselut, Antoine; Kosinski, Michal; West, Robert
Title: Evaluating Language Model Agency through Negotiations
Abstract: We introduce an approach to evaluate language model agency using negotiation games. We test six widely used LLMs evaluating performance and alignment in both self-play and cross-play settings. Only closed-source models were able to complete these tasks and cooperative bargaining games proved most challenging.

Authors: Gandhi, Kanishk; Sadigh, Dorsa; Goodman, Noah D.
Title: Strategic Reasoning with Language Models
Source: NeurIPS 2023 Workshop on Foundation Models for Decision Making
Abstract: This paper introduces an approach using pretrained LLMs with few-shot chain-of-thought examples to enable strategic reasoning for AI agents. Strategies derived from systematically generated prompts generalize almost perfectly to new game structures alternate objectives and hidden information.

Authors: Light, Jonathan; Cai, Min; Shen, Sheng; Hu, Ziniu
Title: AvalonBench: Evaluating LLMs Playing the Game of Avalon
Source: NeurIPS 2023 Workshop on Foundation Models for Decision Making
Abstract: We explore the potential of LLM Agents in playing Resistance Avalon a strategic social deduction game. We introduce AvalonBench a comprehensive game environment for evaluating multi-agent LLM Agents. ChatGPT playing good-role got a win rate of 22.2% against rule-based bots while good-role bot achieves 38.2% win rate.
